summary: Stellar Miller SBD capture of the Oct ’71 show with soaring Dark Star/Sitting‑on‑Top‑of‑the‑World medley and crystal‑clear keyboards.
review: The recording is a superb soundboard (SBD) release – crisp, well‑balanced and featuring Keith Godchaux’s piano front‑and‑center. Musically the concert shines with an inspired Dark Star that melts into Sitting on Top of the World, a high‑energy Truckin' opener, a fresh early version of Tennessee Jed, and a marathon Other One/cryptical suite. Reviewers rave about the band’s interplay, especially Garcia, Weir, Phil and Keith, making this one of the best October ’71 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of detailed reviews (10 out of 12) give 4–5 stars and praise both the performance and the Miller soundboard treatment; only one review is a metadata complaint and another is neutral, resulting in an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller SBD upgrade praised as crystal‑clear with especially prominent piano/keys; no major dropouts or muddiness reported. Minor speaker‑placement quirks noted but not detrimental.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Sitting on Top of the World > Dark Star
show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set packed with new song debuts, followed by a second‑set jam marathon highlighted by an expansive Dark Star medley and tight transitions into Sitting on Top of the World.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as inspired and “sassin’” the whole show, delivering soaring leads especially in Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh is called a “terror” on bass with brilliant interplay throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals/guitar are noted as particularly inspired during the Dark Star/Standing‑on‑Top‑of‑the‑World transition.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux shines, playing to impress; his piano is loud and clear in the mix, a highlight of this Miller treatment.
